artifacts.content:
Mom,

I finally sat down and mapped out my fall schedule at the little table here in Oakridge, and it’s…a lot. I’m going to be at 18 credits: IFMG 352 (Systems Analysis and Design), IFMG 455 (Business Data Communications), MGMT 330 (Production and Operations Management), IFMG 300 (Data Analytics for Business), BTED 350 (Advanced Business Excel Applications), and IFMG 481 (Cloud Computing Fundamentals). Plus the usual 10–12 hours a week at the IT Support Center in Delaney.

I keep thinking back to that appointment at UPMC Shadyside on March 18, 2024 when your oncologist laid out the chemo plan running through late Spring 2025. Sitting in that little exam room in Pittsburgh hearing “Stage II breast cancer” out loud made everything feel way more real than the MyChart messages. That’s honestly why I’m pushing so hard to stay on track for a May 2026 graduation. I want to be done in Indiana by then so I can move back home and actually help with follow‑up stuff instead of being three hours away worrying between classes.

I know an 18‑credit load sounds like I’m trying to kill myself, but I’ve been handling 15–16 pretty well. After that panic attack during my IFMG 250 midterm in Eberly back in March 2023, the CBT I did with Dr. Elena Morris at the IUP Counseling Center (those Tuesday 3:00 p.m. sessions April–July) actually helped. My GPA went from 3.18 to 3.42 while I was in 16 credits, so I feel like I have tools now—structured study blocks, the sleep routine, all that.

I’ll keep my schedule flexible enough that if you need me in Pittsburgh for an infusion day, I can grab the bus from Indiana. Text me after your next Shadyside appointment, okay?

Love,
Jordan

Dad,

Just wanted to loop you in on how I’m planning the next couple semesters. I met with my advisor and we went over DegreeWorks, and the only way to keep graduation in May 2026 is to stack a heavier load. This fall I’m aiming for 18 credits: Systems Analysis and Design, Business Data Communications, Production and Operations Management, Data Analytics for Business, Advanced Business Excel Applications, and the Cloud Computing Fundamentals special topics course.

I keep thinking about when we sat in that PNC Bank branch in Erie back in August 2023 signing the paperwork for the $14,500 private student loan. At the time your job at the Erie manufacturing plant felt solid, and then everything flipped when you got laid off on February 2, 2024. I know the hardship payment plan you worked out with the PNC loan officer only runs through December 2025. That date is burned into my brain.

If I don’t finish on time and get a full‑time MIS or data analyst job soon after May 2026, we’re going to be in a bad spot with that loan. That’s a big part of why I’m okay with pushing myself to 18 credits now instead of dragging things out and risking default later.

Work at the IUP IT Support Center is still 10–12 hours a week. I’m being extra careful there after the whole mess in Stabley last November—customer complaint about that ticket I mishandled in the Stabley Library lab, the performance improvement plan, and the meeting with Mark Ellis on November 21, 2024 where he warned me if my GPA dips below 3.0 they might cut my hours. Another reason to front‑load the MIS and analytics stuff while I know I still have income.

I’ll send you my tentative schedule once registration opens.

Love,
Jordan

Sunday planning – next semester overload

Goal: Stay on track to graduate May 2026 with BS, Management Information Systems + Data Analytics minor.

Credits so far: 96.0 attempted, 93.0 earned, completion rate 96.9%, CGPA 3.42 (up from 3.18 pre‑CBT). I’ve handled 16 credits before (spring last year) while working 10–12 hrs/wk at the IUP IT Support Center.

Plan:
- Register for 18 credits:
  • IFMG 352 – Systems Analysis and Design (3)
  • IFMG 455 – Business Data Communications (3)
  • MGMT 330 – Production and Operations Management (3)
  • IFMG 300 – Data Analytics for Business (3)
  • BTED 350 – Advanced Business Excel Applications (3)
  • IFMG 481 – Special Topics: Cloud Computing Fundamentals (3)

Constraints / reasons:
- Mom: Stage II breast cancer dx at UPMC Shadyside (Pittsburgh) in March 2024, chemo plan through late Spring 2025. Need to be free by May 2026 to move back home and help with follow‑up.
- Dad: co‑signed $14,500 PNC private loan Aug 2023; layoff from Erie manufacturing plant Feb 2, 2024. Hardship payment plan with PNC only through Dec 2025 → need full‑time job ASAP after graduation to avoid default.
- Ashley: shoplifting incident at Millcreek Mall June 2024; juvenile court hearing scheduled Oct 9, 2025 at Erie County Courthouse. Parents want me back in Erie to help keep an eye on her once I’m done at IUP.
- Work: on performance improvement plan after Nov 2024 Stabley Library ticket complaint; Mark Ellis warned Nov 21, 2024 that if GPA <3.0 while working 10–12 hrs/wk, hours may be cut.

Supports:
- CBT tools from 12‑week program with Dr. Elena Morris at IUP Counseling Center (Tuesdays 3:00–3:50 p.m., April–July 2023): structured study blocks, exposure to timed exams, sleep routine.
- Google Calendar: block out all class, work, and study time; color‑code exams in red; weekly Trello review Sundays 7–9 p.m.

Action items:
- Email Megan/Mark re: keeping work schedule flexible around exams.
- Check in with Mom after next Shadyside appointment.
- Call Dad to confirm PNC hardship plan end date.
- Ask professors early about major project due dates to avoid overlap.

Mark P: You alive? Haven’t seen you in the lab all week

Jordan: Barely lol. Been camped in Eberly trying to make this 18‑credit thing workable.

Mark P: Wait you’re really doing 18??

Jordan: Yeah. Systems Analysis, Business Data Comm, Ops Mgmt, Data Analytics, Advanced Excel, and that Cloud Computing Fundamentals special topics. All in one shot.

Mark P: Dude why

Jordan: Combo of stuff. Mom’s Stage II breast cancer treatments at UPMC Shadyside are supposed to run through late Spring 2025. I want to be done by May 2026 so I can move back home and not be stuck in Indiana an extra semester while she’s still doing follow‑ups.

Jordan: Plus the money side. Remember when Dad got laid off from the Erie plant last Feb 2? He co‑signed that $14.5k PNC private loan back in Aug 2023 and the hardship payment plan with the PNC branch in Erie only goes through Dec 2025. If I drag school out, we’re screwed.

Mark P: That’s rough man :/

Jordan: And work’s been weird. After that complaint in the Stabley Library lab in November, Mark Ellis put me on a performance improvement plan. In our Nov 21, 2024 meeting he straight up said if my GPA drops below 3.0 while I’m doing 10–12 hrs a week, they might cut my hours. So I’d rather front‑load the MIS/Data Analytics courses now while I know I still have the job.

Mark P: You sure you can handle it though?

Jordan: I think so. The CBT I did with Dr. Morris at the IUP Counseling Center after that IFMG 250 panic attack actually helped. My GPA jumped from 3.18 to 3.42 while I was in 16 credits. Gonna lean hard on Google Calendar and lo‑fi beats.

Mark P: Ok spreadsheet king, I trust you. Let me know when you need a Mario Kart break.

Jordan: Deal.

Event: Weekly therapy – Dr. Elena Morris (test anxiety / overload prep)

Date: Tuesdays, recurring (April 11, 2023 – July 4, 2023)
Time: 3:00–3:50 p.m.
Location: Indiana University of Pennsylvania Counseling Center, 2nd floor
Attendee: Jordan A. Reynolds (@01234567)

Description / notes:
- Started these sessions after panic attack during IFMG 250 midterm in Eberly Hall in March 2023 (couldn’t breathe halfway through the exam, had to step into hallway with proctor).
- 12‑week CBT program with Dr. Elena Morris focused on:
  • Structured study blocks for programming and MIS courses
  • Gradual exposure to timed exams
  • Sleep and pre‑exam routines
  • Cognitive restructuring around grades / perfectionism
- Goal: build skills so I can handle heavier loads (16–18 credits) in later semesters while working 10–12 hrs/wk at IUP IT Support Center.

Talking points to bring up:
- Upcoming plan to take 18 credits (IFMG 352, IFMG 455, MGMT 330, IFMG 300, BTED 350, IFMG 481) so I can finish MIS + Data Analytics on time.
- Family stress: Mom’s new Stage II breast cancer diagnosis at UPMC Shadyside (appointment March 18, 2024 where oncologist said chemo likely through late Spring 2025); Dad’s layoff from Erie manufacturing plant on Feb 2, 2024 and $14,500 PNC private loan hardship plan only through Dec 2025.
- Worry about Ashley’s shoplifting incident at Millcreek Mall (June 2024) and upcoming juvenile court hearing at Erie County Courthouse on Oct 9, 2025; parents asking me to move home after IUP.
- Pressure from performance improvement plan at IT Support Center after mishandled ticket in Stabley Library lab and Nov 21, 2024 meeting with Mark Ellis re: keeping GPA ≥3.0.

Reminder: leave Eberly by 2:45 p.m., bring notebook with weekly schedule drafts.

Hey Ash,

How’s senior year chaos? Mom said you’re bouncing between IUP and Pitt in your head every other day.

Things here are the usual mix of boring and stressful. I’m lining up a pretty heavy semester: Systems Analysis and Design, Business Data Communications, Ops Management, Data Analytics for Business, Advanced Business Excel, and that Cloud Computing Fundamentals class. It comes out to 18 credits, which is nuts, but I kind of have to if I want to walk in May 2026.

Part of it is you, actually. I’ve been thinking about what Mom and Dad said after the whole Millcreek Mall thing last June. I know the shoplifting incident in June 2024 was a stupid mistake and you’ve heard that a million times, but the juvenile court hearing at the Erie County Courthouse on October 9, 2025 is a big deal. They really do want me back in Erie as soon as I’m done at IUP to help keep an eye on you and give them a break.

Add that to Mom’s Stage II breast cancer stuff at UPMC Shadyside—remember when we were all in that exam room in Pittsburgh on March 18, 2024 and her oncologist walked through chemo going into late Spring 2025—and it just feels like I need to be home, not hanging around Indiana for an extra semester because I couldn’t get Systems Analysis and Business Data Comm done together.

I’m not mad at you about any of this, btw. You’re allowed to screw up and figure stuff out. I just want to be there when you do, not three hours away refreshing my phone between classes.

Text me after your next college visit. If you come to IUP, I’m making you carry my backpack for a day so you see what 18 credits feels like.

Love,
Jordan

Hi Mark,

I wanted to touch base about my schedule for next fall and make sure it still works with my shifts at the IT Support Center.

I’m planning to enroll in 18 credits so I can complete my remaining MIS and Data Analytics requirements on time: IFMG 352 (Systems Analysis and Design), IFMG 455 (Business Data Communications), MGMT 330 (Production and Operations Management), IFMG 300 (Data Analytics for Business), BTED 350 (Advanced Business Excel Applications), and IFMG 481 (Cloud Computing Fundamentals). Most of these are upper‑level courses with group projects and labs, so I’m trying to be realistic about my availability.

I’m currently working 10–12 hours per week between Delaney and the Stabley Library lab. After the complaint in Stabley last November about the ticket I mishandled, and the performance improvement plan we set up, I’ve been trying to be extra intentional about how I balance work and classes. I’ve thought a lot about our November 21, 2024 meeting where you mentioned that if my GPA dropped below 3.0 while I was working those hours, there could be a reduction in shifts.

The good news is that the CBT work I did with Dr. Elena Morris at the IUP Counseling Center after that panic attack during my IFMG 250 midterm in March 2023 has made a big difference. My cumulative GPA is up to 3.42, and I’ve been using the structured study blocks and scheduling tools we talked about to stay on top of things.

Would it be possible to keep me mostly on afternoon and early evening shifts Monday–Thursday, and avoid stacking me on nights before major exam days once I have my syllabi? I’m happy to send you my finalized class schedule and a draft work availability grid as soon as registration goes through.

Thanks for working with me on this.

Best,
Jordan

metadata.subject: Fall course load and work schedule
